formations_blocks: Portugal sustained possession/attacking phase against a set defence
DR Congo stepping up quickly as a unit when Portugal have the ball in their own half
key_matchups: Cancelo’s low cross across goal with no Portugal runner able to finish it
Ronaldo looking for early service and aerial duels against the DRC back line
phases.summary: Portugal began by keeping possession from the kickoff and immediately established an attack-vs-defence pattern, with DR Congo sitting in a back five and pressing up quickly only once Portugal entered their own half. Portugal’s movement was more inventive than a simple crossing game, exemplified by Cancelo’s low delivery across the face of goal, and they were rewarded with Neves’ early opener on 6 minutes.

formations_blocks: DRC in a pressing setup that, when activated, leaves space in behind
adjustments: Bernardo potentially playing deeper in future matches is suggested
Portugal’s attack shows a possible 4-3-1-2 idea with Bruno as the 1 and Neto alongside him in the 2
key_matchups: Nuno Mendes vs DRC defender(s) on the burst into the box
Wan-Bissaka vs Nuno Mendes on the sliding recovery tackle
Neto vs DRC defender(s) on the right-side break
Ronaldo as target for Neto’s low cross
phases.summary: Portugal are comfortably controlling possession and were described as being allowed to knock it about, with the main tactical risk being the space behind if DRC press higher. The commentary also hints at Portugal’s long-term structural options, suggesting Bernardo Silva deeper and a 4-3-1-2 with Bruno Fernandes central and Neto alongside him, while their width is currently being supplied by the full-backs.

The half-time state is level at 1-1, with the commentary framing the contrast between Portugal’s and DR Congo’s goals rather than describing any specific tactical structure. The only clear tactical takeaway is an aesthetic one: DR Congo’s goal is presented as a clean, well-worked finish, while Portugal’s is described as more of a scrappier set-piece-style effort.

formations_blocks: Portugal chasing the game in a more direct, wide-attacking setup after the break
DRC settling into a compact midfield phase before breaking forward on the counter
DRC winning corners and looking dangerous from set pieces
adjustments: Martinez sends on Conceicao for Bernardo, prioritizing dribbling and speed on the outside
Portugal get Bruno Fernandes taking short/set-piece delivery aimed at Ronaldo’s movement
Portugal use a wide left combination with Mendes overlapping and Ronaldo combining inside
DRC’s attacks increasingly come through right-sided midfield carry and box entry
key_matchups: Mendes and Ronaldo vs Tuanzebe and Kapaudi
phases.summary: Portugal re-emerged from the interval with a clear emphasis on width and pace, highlighted by Martinez bringing on Conceicao for Bernardo to attack outside spaces. They created several promising chances through wide crosses, set pieces and overlapping left-side combinations, but DRC stayed competitive, using midfield retention and quick breaks to threaten at the other end. The phase was defined by Portugal’s increased directness and DRC’s resilience, with both sides producing moments in the box without a clean breakthrough.

formations_blocks: Portugal attacking phase with crosses and direct balls
Portugal two-striker attacking setup after substitutions
adjustments: Portugal bring on Goncalo Ramos for Vitinha, shifting to a front two
DRC replace Wan-Bissaka and Bakambu with Kalulu and Banza
Portugal increasingly use over-the-top passes and low crosses to attack DRC's box
DRC continue to counter whenever possible rather than sit purely in a rearguard
key_matchups: Bruno Fernandes vs DRC's defensive block
phases.summary: DRC hold a disciplined, compact defensive structure while still threatening on the counter, with the text stressing their confidence and ability to spring forward whenever space appears. Portugal respond by pushing more direct, including a switch to a front two and a stream of crosses and lofted passes, but DRC repeatedly absorb the pressure and win the individual duels at the back.
